```text
21	I am a first-year **Ph.D. student** in the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering at the <img src="../assets/img/uwmadison.png" class="inline-logo" alt="UW–Madison"/>[University of Wisconsin-Madison](https://www.wisc.edu/) (UW-Madison). I joined <img src="../assets/img/skylab.png" class="inline-logo" alt="Sky-Lab"/>[Sky-Lab](https://sky-lab-uw.github.io/) at UW-Madison in Fall 2025 under the guidance of Dr.[Sikai (Sky) Chen](https://sky-lab-uw.github.io/people/). On Wisconsin!<img src="../assets/img/bucky.png" class="inline-logo" alt="Bucky"/>
22	
23	Before joining UW-Madison, I obtained my M.S. in Transportation Engineering at [College of Transportation](https://tjjt.tongji.edu.cn/), <img src="../assets/img/tongji.png" class="inline-logo" alt="Tongji"/>Tongji University in 2025, and my B.E. in Vehicle Engineering at [Institute of Rail Transit](https://railway.tongji.edu.cn/main.htm), Tongji University in 2022. I am very fortunate and honored to be advised by Prof. [Hechao Zhou](https://tjjt.tongji.edu.cn/info/2943/10933.htm) and Prof. [Jimin Zhang](https://railway.tongji.edu.cn/c5/e6/c4609a50662/page.htm) during my study at Tongji University. 🫶
24	
25	As for now, I am working to accelerate the development of future intelligent transportation technologies. 🧐 Specifically, I focus on:
26	
27	- **Autonomous driving**
28	- **Vision-Language-Action model**
29	- **Intelligent transportation systems**
30	
31	An equally important dimension of my work is to validate autonomous driving related methods through **real‑vehicle testing**: I have contributed to the development of <img src="../assets/img/Sky-Drive.png" class="inline-logo" alt="SkyDrive"/>[SkyDrive](https://github.com/BillWan-zzzyyy/Sky-Drive), an open source multi-agent platform for future transportation system and autonomous driving by Sky-Lab.
```

```bash
"
```

```text
from PIL import Image
```

```bash
        print('  P (paletted) - converting to RGBA to inspect')
        rgba = img.convert('RGBA')
        alpha = rgba.split()[3]
        extrema = alpha.getextrema()
        print('  alpha extrema:', extrema)
        print('  top-left pixel:', rgba.getpixel((0,0)))
        print('  top-right pixel:', rgba.getpixel((rgba.size[0]-1, 0)))
"
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-25 20:38:12Z)**_

Both PNGs have fully opaque white backgrounds. I'll use Pillow to flood-fill the connected white from each corner with full transparency — this preserves the white inside Bucky (the chest "W", face fur) while erasing only the surrounding background.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-25 20:38:21Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```python
"""One-off: replace solid white backgrounds with transparency for bucky.png and Sky-Drive.png.

Flood-fills from each corner so any white *inside* the figure (e.g. Bucky's chest W) is preserved.
Then re-multiplies anti-aliased edge pixels by their distance from white to soften halos.
"""

from pathlib import Path
from PIL import Image, ImageDraw

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
TARGETS = [ROOT / "assets/img/bucky.png", ROOT / "assets/img/Sky-Drive.png"]
THRESH = 30  # treat near-white as background to catch anti-aliased edges


def make_transparent(path: Path) -> None:
    img = Image.open(path).convert("RGBA")
    w, h = img.size
    for corner in [(0, 0), (w - 1, 0), (0, h - 1), (w - 1, h - 1)]:
        ImageDraw.floodfill(img, corner, (255, 255, 255, 0), thresh=THRESH)

    # Soften residual halo: where pixels are still nearly-white and bordering transparency,
    # scale alpha down by distance-from-white so edges fade cleanly.
    px = img.load()
    for y in range(h):
        for x in range(w):
            r, g, b, a = px[x, y]
            if a == 0:
                continue
            # distance from white in [0, 255*3]; small => near-white => more transparent
            d = (255 - r) + (255 - g) + (255 - b)
            if d < 60:  # near-white
                px[x, y] = (r, g, b, min(a, int(d * (255 / 60))))

    img.save(path, optimize=True)
    print(f"wrote {path.relative_to(ROOT)}")


for p in TARGETS:
    make_transparent(p)

```

```text
File created successfully at: /Users/jamesyoung/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UW-Madison/My_Github/BillWan-zzzyyy.github.io/_scripts/_oneoff_transparent_bg.py
```
</details></tool-use>

Verify alpha channels post-conversion

```bash
python3 -c "
from PIL import Image
for f in ['assets/img/bucky.png', 'assets/img/Sky-Drive.png']:
    img = Image.open(f)
    print(f, 'mode:', img.mode, 'size:', img.size)
    alpha = img.split()[-1]

    print('  alpha extrema:', extrema)
    print('  corners (r,g,b,a):')
    print('   tl:', img.getpixel((0,0)))
    print('   tr:', img.getpixel((img.size[0]-1, 0)))
    print('   bl:', img.getpixel((0, img.size[1]-1)))
    print('   br:', img.getpixel((img.size[0]-1, img.size[1]-1)))
    # count transparent pixels
    transparent = sum(1 for p in alpha.getdata() if p == 0)
    total = img.size[0]*img.size[1]
    print(f'  fully-transparent pixels: {transparent}/{total} ({100*transparent/total:.1f}%)')
"

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Composite onto blue to inspect transparency edges

```bash
python3 -c "
from PIL import Image
for f, out in [('assets/img/bucky.png', '/tmp/bucky_check.png'), ('assets/img/Sky-Drive.png', '/tmp/sky_check.png')]:
    img = Image.open(f).convert('RGBA')
    bg = Image.new('RGBA', img.size, (60, 130, 200, 255))  # blue
    bg.paste(img, (0,0), img)
    bg.convert('RGB').save(out)
    print('wrote', out)
"

```

```text
wrote /tmp/sky_check.png
```

<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Compare Bucky pixels before vs after for internal whites

```bash
python3 -c "
from PIL import Image
img = Image.open('/tmp/bucky.bak.png').convert('RGBA')
out = Image.open('assets/img/bucky.png')
w, h = img.size
print(f'Size: {w}x{h}')
# Sample known-internal face positions on the ORIGINAL vs OUTPUT
samples = [
    ('forehead between ears', w//2, int(h*0.10)),
    ('between eyes', w//2, int(h*0.18)),
    ('left cheek', int(w*0.30), int(h*0.25)),
    ('right cheek', int(w*0.70), int(h*0.25)),
    ('snout', w//2, int(h*0.30)),
    ('chest below face', w//2, int(h*0.40)),
    ('center of W on sweater', int(w*0.55), int(h*0.55)),
]
for name, x, y in samples:
    a = img.getpixel((x, y))
    b = out.getpixel((x, y))
    print(f'  {name:30s} (x={x},y={y}) orig={a}  ->  out={b}')
"

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Try threshold=0 floodfill for Bucky

```bash
python3 -c "
from PIL import Image, ImageDraw
img = Image.open('assets/img/bucky.png').convert('RGBA')
w, h = img.size
# threshold=0: only fill exact white
for corner in [(0,0),(w-1,0),(0,h-1),(w-1,h-1)]:
    ImageDraw.floodfill(img, corner, (255,255,255,0), thresh=0)
# composite onto blue to inspect
bg = Image.new('RGBA', (w,h), (60,130,200,255))
bg.paste(img, (0,0), img)
bg.convert('RGB').save('/tmp/bucky_t0.png')
print('alpha extrema:', img.split()[-1].getextrema())
# sample face positions
for name, (x,y) in [('forehead', (w//2, 30)), ('between_eyes', (w//2, 54)), ('chest_W', (128, 165))]:
    print(f'  {name}: {img.getpixel((x,y))}')"

```

```text
alpha extrema: (0, 255)
  forehead: (255, 255, 255, 255)
  between_eyes: (255, 255, 255, 255)
  chest_W: (255, 255, 255, 255)
```

Re-run script and composite for visual check

```bash
python3 _scripts/_oneoff_transparent_bg.py && python3 -c "
from PIL import Image
for f, out in [('assets/img/bucky.png', '/tmp/bucky_check.png'), ('assets/img/Sky-Drive.png', '/tmp/sky_check.png')]:
    img = Image.open(f).convert('RGBA')
    bg = Image.new('RGBA', img.size, (60, 130, 200, 255))
    bg.paste(img, (0,0), img)
    bg.convert('RGB').save(out)
    a_extr = img.split()[-1].getextrema()
    print(f'{f}  alpha extrema={a_extr}')
"

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-25 20:41:29Z)**_

Done. Both `assets/img/bucky.png` and `assets/img/Sky-Drive.png` now have transparent backgrounds (RGBA with alpha 0–255):

- Connected outside white → fully transparent via flood-fill from each corner with `thresh=0`.
- Anti-aliased near-white edge pixels are alpha-faded so the outlines blend cleanly into any page background.
- Interior whites preserved: Bucky's face fur, the chest "W", belly fur, and (for Sky-Drive) the design/text are unchanged.

`about.md` itself didn't need editing — the `<img class="inline-logo">` tags will pick up the new transparent PNGs on next build. Verified by compositing onto a blue test background; only the surrounding box disappeared.
